get.description: Get current license info.

post.description: Activate a license key.
Remove the activated license (revert to free tier).

Generate a new license key.
In production, this endpoint should be removed or require admin auth.
For now, useful for testing.

Register a new user (admin-only).

First user (when no users exist) can self-register as admin.
Login with username/password and receive JWT token.
Logout (client must delete token - stateless JWT).

get.description: Get current authenticated user's info.

post.description: Refresh JWT token (extend expiry).

get.description: List all users (admin-only).

Get user details.

Users can view their own profile, admins can view anyone.

put.description:
Update user profile.

Users can update their own email/full_name.
Admins can update anyone's role/email/full_name.

delete.description:
Delete a user (admin-only).

Cannot delete yourself (prevents lockout).

patch.description:
Change user password.

Users must provide current_password to change their own.
Admins can reset anyone's password without current_password.

Activate or deactivate a user account (admin-only).

Prevents user from logging in without deleting their data.
Update node fields: name, config (startup config), x, y position.

post.description:
Start a node. Branches on node.type:
- 'docker': pulls image (if needed), ensures lab network, runs container.
- everything else (with console_type=='vnc'): spawns QEMU and assigns VNC port.
- PTY nodes: just flips status to running.

Stop a node. For docker nodes, removes the container and tears down the
lab network if no other docker nodes in the same lab are still running.
For VNC nodes, kills the QEMU process.
Store RDP host/port for a node and mark it ready.

get.description: List all networks, optionally filtered by lab_id.

post.description: Create a new network (bridge or NAT).

get.description: Get details of a specific network.

delete.description: Delete a network and clean up infrastructure.

get.description: Check health status of a NAT network.

patch.description: Update quality settings for a link (delay, jitter, loss, bandwidth, style).

post.description:
Start packet capture on a network link.

Requires tcpdump with CAP_NET_RAW capability:
    sudo setcap cap_net_raw,cap_net_admin=eip $(which tcpdump)

BPF filter examples:
    - "tcp port 80" (HTTP traffic)
    - "icmp" (ping packets)
    - "host 192.168.1.1" (traffic to/from specific IP)
    - "" (capture everything)
Stop an active packet capture.

get.description: List all active packet captures.

Download PCAP file for a capture.

Can be opened in Wireshark, tcpdump, or tshark.

post.description: Delete PCAP files older than max_age_hours (default: 24).

get.description: List all templates with optional filtering.

post.description: Create a new template (user-uploaded images only, is_builtin=0).

get.description: Get template details by ID.

put.description:
Update template metadata.

Built-in templates (is_builtin=1) can only update visibility and icon.
User templates can update everything.

delete.description:
Delete a user template.

Built-in templates (is_builtin=1) cannot be deleted.

post.description:
Upload a QEMU/KVM disk image (qcow2, vmdk, ova).

Returns the file path to use in template creation.
Supports chunked upload for large files (vendor images can be 1-10GB).

get.description: List all unique template categories.
List all unique vendors.

Check for a newer OmniLab version. Honors the configured frequency
unless force=true. Returns cached result if checked recently.
Get current auto-update check settings.

post.description: Change update check preferences.

Hide the update notification for this specific version. User can still
see it via System -> Updates, but the corner badge stops nagging.

One-session dismissal. The frontend tracks this in sessionStorage,
so this endpoint is purely a no-op acknowledgment.
Create a .omnilab archive of all OmniLab state.
Inspect a .omnilab archive without applying it.
Restore from a .omnilab archive. Be careful - this can overwrite data.

get.description: Combined system + backend process + API health metrics.
Docker daemon stats.
Network: OVS bridges, interfaces.
Lab and node statistics from the database.

Check image directory permissions (CRE-53 enhancement).

Verifies all images are readable/writable by the service user.
EVE-NG requires manual 'fixpermissions' after uploads - OmniLab auto-fixes!

post.description:
Force fix all image permissions (admin only in production).

Sets all images to 644 (rw-r--r--) with service user ownership.
Unlike EVE-NG, this is rarely needed - upload API sets correct perms automatically.

get.description: Whether the install has completed initial setup.

post.description:
Persist the wizard's choices.

Idempotency rule: once `complete` is True we refuse — see module docstring.

post.description:
Dispatch a single tool by name. Returns the standard envelope.

Unknown tool -> VALIDATION error envelope (404-style, but kept in-envelope so the
agent in CRE-44 sees a uniform shape). Tool-raised AILBError -> error envelope.

schema.title: Response Call Tool Api Agent Tools  Name  Post

get.description: Discovery endpoint — names the agent loop (CRE-44) can introspect.

schema.title: Response List Tools Api Agent Tools Get

post.description:
Run the lab-builder agent for ``prompt`` and stream its events as SSE.

Each event from ``agent_runner.run_build`` is serialized as one SSE frame
(``data: {json}\n\n``). The runner enforces the CRE-40 cost rails and
redacts the API key from any error it surfaces.

CRE-47: a ``run_id`` is assigned and an agent_runs row is persisted at start
(status='running'). The run_id is emitted to the client via the runner's
early ``run_started`` event so the frontend knows what to /cancel. On the
terminal event (done/cancelled/error) the row is updated with the final
status, lab_id, tool_call_count and total_tokens.

Request graceful cancellation of an in-flight build.

Sets the cancel flag in ``agent_runner``'s module-level registry; the
(sync) build generator polls it between iterations/tool-calls and stops,
tearing down any partially-built lab and emitting a terminal ``cancelled``
event (which updates the agent_runs row). Idempotent: cancelling an unknown
or already-finished run simply reports ``cancelled: false``.

schema.title: Response Cancel Build Api Agent Build  Run Id  Cancel Post

get.description: Return the last 10 runs (most recent first) for the history UI.

schema.title: Response List Runs Api Agent Runs Get

get.description:
Return the stored AI-provider config WITHOUT the api_key.

Fresh install with no key -> ``api_key_set: false`` so the UI can treat the
AI builder as disabled and prompt the operator to configure a provider.

schema.title: Response Get Ai Provider Api Settings Ai Provider Get

post.description:
Persist the AI-provider config. The api_key is encrypted at rest and is
NEVER echoed back — the response is the same redacted shape as GET.

schema.title: Response Update Ai Provider Api Settings Ai Provider Post

post.description:
Ping the configured provider. Returns {ok, latency_ms, model} on success
or {ok: false, error} on failure. Never makes a real call in tests (the
``_ping_provider`` seam is mocked). The api_key never appears in output.

schema.title: Response Test Ai Provider Api Agent Test Post
